headlineSummary: Gemini drew clearly negative comments about practical usefulness [E3], while Anthropic had mixed feedback with both strong praise and trust concerns [E1][E2].

ranking.summary: Limited evidence points negative, with one comment saying Gemini is far from practically useful and cannot complete the task end to end [E3].
Anthropic was mixed-neutral: one commenter praised Claude Code on difficult problems, while another criticized Fable’s silent guardrails and trust issues [E1][E2].

evidence.excerpt: in Claude Code (and Claude.ai and Claude Code for web) to have an opinion on Fable 5: it's a beast. I'm throwing some VERY difficult problems at at - things I've been dragging my heels on for months - and it's crunching through them very happily.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly references Claude products and strongly praises Fable 5's capability on difficult problems.

evidence.excerpt: This makes Fable unusable for me. If I cannot tell whether I am paying for the whole service or just a partial one, because somehow their guardrails have decided my work silently broke their terms of service, then I prefer to go to older models or alternatives

annotations.rationale: In the Claude Fable story context, the commenter says Fable is unusable because silent guardrails undermine trust in the paid service.

evidence.excerpt: It's just nowhere near practical utility yet. Not "nearly there" but "not nearly half way there". I got the top tier of Gemini AI. Can it rent me a car? "As an AI I can absolutely guide you through the process of renting the car, but I can't physically access the web site or type in the details for you".

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly names Gemini AI and criticizes it as far from practically useful because it cannot complete the intended task.
